Title: Cyclin-dependent kinase activity enhances phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis in Arabidopsis by repressing phosphatidic acid phosphohydrolase activity

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lipin-family phosphatidic acid phosphohydrolase; haloacid dehalogenase-like phosphatase; N-terminal amphipathic helix predicted
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: PA-to-DAG step controlling flux into membrane phospholipid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dephosphorylates phosphatidic acid to form diacylglycerol and inorganic phosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major membrane phospholipid required for endomembrane biogenesis and normal cell division/growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PAH1 represses phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis; CDKA;1-mediated phosphorylation of PAH1 reduces PAH1 activity and membrane association, thereby enhancing PC biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAH1 is the predominant PAH isoform in developing Arabidopsis leaves, as pah1 single mutants show reduced PAH activity and elevated PC biosynthesis. 2. Reintroduction of PAH1 into pah1 pah2 and pah1 pah2 cdka;1D backgrounds restores high PAH activity and suppresses PC biosynthesis. 3. CDK-cyclin complexes phosphorylate PAH1 at serine 162 in vitro, reducing PAH activity. 4. A non-phosphorylatable PAH1S162A variant shows higher activity, greater membrane association, lower PC biosynthesis, and reduced cell division rate in leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Overlapping with PAH2 in Arabidopsis; predominant functional isoform in developing rosette leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing rosette leaves / first true leaves
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predominantly soluble/cytosolic; minor microsomal membrane association; non-phosphorylatable S162A form shows increased membrane association
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedlings and plants grown under 22°C 16 h light / 18°C 8 h dark conditions; leaf radiolabeling with [methyl-14C]choline for PC biosynthesis assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis; transgenic complementation with Pro35S:PAH1-HA; site-directed mutagenesis (S162A); in vitro kinase assay; LC-MS/MS phosphosite mapping; PAH enzyme assay; subcellular fractionation; immunoblotting; radiolabeled choline incorporation into PC; leaf kinematic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established for PAH1 itself; acts as a key control point upstream of CCT-mediated stimulation of PC synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Expression of non-phosphorylatable PAH1S162A reduces PC biosynthesis and decreases leaf cell division rate, causing smaller mature leaves with fewer but larger cells.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: PAH1 phosphoregulation could be used to tune membrane phospholipid production and growth, but constitutively active/dephosphorylated PAH1 risks reduced cell proliferation and smaller leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS phosphoproteomic analysis of in vitro phosphorylated PAH1; phosphosite support from PHOSPHAT 4.0 database
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Serine 162 is the only CDK-consensus PAH1 phosphosite identified here and is supported by in vivo phosphoproteomics, but additional phosphorylation sites may exist. Phosphorylation decreases both catalytic activity and membrane residency.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lipin-family phosphatidic acid phosphohydrolase; predicted N-terminal amphipathic helix
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dephosphorylates phosphatidic acid to form diacylglycerol and inorganic phosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major membrane phospholipid required for endomembrane biogenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PAH2 overlaps with PAH1 in repressing PC biosynthesis, but plays a less prominent role than PAH1 in leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAH2 overlaps functionally with PAH1 in Arabidopsis leaves. 2. pah2 single mutants show no obvious morphological phenotype and less impact than pah1. 3. Double mutant pah1 pah2 strongly alters PAH activity and PC biosynthesis, showing redundancy. 4. PAH2 may also be phosphorylated, but this was not directly tested functionally in the paper.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Likely predominantly soluble with membrane-associated fraction inferred from family behavior; not directly tested here
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Same leaf growth and radiolabel assay conditions as study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single- and double-mutant analysis; PAH enzyme assay; radiolabeled choline incorporation into PC
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible redundancy partner to PAH1 for engineering phospholipid metabolism, but weaker individual effect in leaves suggests limited standalone utility.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Reference to PHOSPHAT 4.0 evidence for phosphorylation at several sites including S524
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAH2 was not the main mechanistic focus of the study; included because it is a direct experimental subject in mutant analyses affecting PC biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: cdka;1D and cdka;1DE weak-allele lines in Arabidopsis background
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Phospholipid biosynthesis via post-translational repression of phosphatidic acid phosphohydrolase activity
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major membrane phospholipid required for endomembrane biogenesis and normal cell division.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CDKA;1 enhances phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis by repressing PAH activity through phosphorylation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Weak cdka;1 alleles show ~50% higher PAH activity and ~30% lower PC biosynthesis in leaves than wild type. 2. The PC biosynthesis defect of cdka;1D is abolished in the pah1 pah2 background, showing dependence on PAH. 3. CDK-cyclin complexes phosphorylate PAH1 at serine 162 in vitro and this reduces PAH activity. 4. In vivo, reduced CDKA;1 activity lowers PAH1 phosphorylation, supporting direct control of phospholipid metabolism by the cell-cycle kinase.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in CDKA;1-cyclin complexes purified on p13Suc1-Sepharose
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core cell-cycle regulator that positively drives PC biosynthesis by inhibiting the opposing PAH branch through phosphorylation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Phosphorylates and inhibits PAH1; likely suppresses PAH1-mediated diversion of phosphatidic acid away from PC-promoting flux
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Post-translational phosphorylation-mediated inhibition of enzyme activity and membrane association
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAH1; likely PAH2 not functionally demonstrated
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cell-cycle progression cues; specific upstream inputs not dissected in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Core cell-cycle regulator active during leaf growth and development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing rosette leaves / early leaf development
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native mutant alleles; no transgenic promoter used for core CDKA;1 effect assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard leaf growth conditions; interpretation linked to early leaf development and cell division
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Weak-allele mutant analysis (cdka;1D, cdka;1DE); genetic interaction with pah1 pah2; in vitro kinase assay; phospho-immunoblotting with MPM2; LC-MS/MS phosphosite mapping; PC radiolabeling; PAH enzyme assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced CDKA;1 activity causes severe growth retardation/defects and lower PC content; growth defects are not rescued by relieving repression of phospholipid synthesis in pah1 pah2 background.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful mechanistic target for coupling membrane biogenesis with proliferation, but direct manipulation is likely impractical due to broad essential roles in the cell cycle and strong growth penalties.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS phosphosite mapping of PAH1 after CDK treatment
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study uses reduced-activity phosphomimic alleles cdka;1D and cdka;1DE rather than nulls because null mutants are severely growth retarded. CDKA;1 acts through PAH-dependent suppression rather than direct activation of PC biosynthetic enzymes shown here.
